v.CNV: 那时，希律王下手苦害教会中的几个人，
v.ESV: About that time Herod the king laid violent hands on some who belonged to the church.
v.GAE: 그 때에 헤롯 왕이 손을 들어 교회 중에서 몇 사람을 해하려 하여
v.KJV: Now about that time Herod the king stretched forth his hands to vex certain of the church.
v.KRV: 그 때에 헤롯왕이 손을 들어 교회 중 몇 사람을 해하려하여
v.NASB: Now about that time Herod the king laid hands on some who belonged to the church in order to mistreat them.
v.NIV: It was about this time that King Herod arrested some who belonged to the church, intending to persecute them.
v.NRSV: About that time King Herod laid violent hands upon some who belonged to the church.
v.SAENEW: 이 무렵에 헤롯 왕이 손을 뻗쳐서, 교회에 속한 몇몇 사람을 해하였다.
v.WLB: 그 무렵 헤롯 왕이 교회를 박해하려고 교회에 속한 몇몇 사람들을 체포했습니다.

v.CNV: 用刀杀了约翰的哥哥雅各。
v.ESV: He killed James the brother of John with the sword,
v.GAE: 요한의 형제 야고보를 칼로 죽이니
v.KJV: And he killed James the brother of John with the sword.
v.KRV: 요한의 형제 야고보를 칼로 죽이니
v.NASB: And he had James the brother of John put to death with a sword.
v.NIV: He had James, the brother of John, put to death with the sword.
v.NRSV: He had James, the brother of John, killed with the sword.
v.SAENEW: 그는 먼저 요한과 형제간인 야고보를 칼로 죽였다.
v.WLB: 헤롯은 먼저 요한의 형제 야고보를 칼로 죽였습니다.

v.CNV: 他见这事博得了犹太人的欢心，就在除酵节期间，再次捉拿彼得。
v.ESV: and when he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. This was during the days of Unleavened Bread.
v.GAE: 유대인들이 이 일을 기뻐하는 것을 보고 베드로도 잡으려 할새 때는 무교절 기간이라
v.KJV: And because he saw it pleased the Jews, he proceeded further to take Peter also. (Then were the days of unleavened bread.)
v.KRV: 유대인들이 이 일을 기뻐하는 것을 보고 베드로도 잡으려 할쌔 때는 무교절일이라
v.NASB: When he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. Now it was during the days of Unleavened Bread.
v.NIV: When he saw that this met with approval among the Jews, he proceeded to seize Peter also. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
v.NRSV: After he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. (This was during the festival of Unleavened Bread.)
v.SAENEW: 헤롯은 유대 사람들이 이 일을 기뻐하는 것을 보고, 이제는 베드로까지 잡으려고 하였다. 때는 무교절 기간이었다.
v.WLB: 이 일을 유대 사람들이 기뻐하는 것을 본 헤롯은 이어 베드로도 잡아들였습니다. 이때는 무교절 기간이었습니다.

v.CNV: 捉到了，就把他关在监里，交给四班士兵看守，每班四个人，打算过了逾越节，把他提出来交给民众。
v.ESV: And when he had seized him, he put him in prison, delivering him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him, intending after the Passover to bring him out to the people.
v.GAE: 잡으매 옥에 가두어 군인 넷씩인 네 패에게 맡겨 지키고 유월절 후에 백성 앞에 끌어 내고자 하더라
v.KJV: And when he had apprehended him, he put him in prison, and delivered him to four quaternions of soldiers to keep him; intending after Easter to bring him forth to the people.
v.KRV: 잡으매 옥에 가두어 군사 넷씩인 네 패에게 맡겨 지키고 유월절 후에 백성 앞에 끌어 내고자 하더라
v.NASB: When he had seized him, he put him in prison, delivering him to four squads of soldiers to guard him, intending after the Passover to bring him out before the people.
v.NIV: After arresting him, he put him in prison, handing him over to be guarded by four squads of four soldiers each. Herod intended to bring him out for public trial after the Passover.
v.NRSV: When he had seized him, he put him in prison and handed him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him, intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover.
v.SAENEW: 그는 베드로도 잡아서 감옥에 가두고, 네 명으로 짠 경비병 네 패에게 맡겨서 지키게 하였다. 유월절이 지나면, 백성들 앞에 그를 끌어낼 속셈이었다.
v.WLB: 그는 베드로를 잡아 감옥에 가두고 군인들을 네 명씩 한 조를 지어서 네 조가 지키도록 했습니다. 헤롯은 유월절이 지나면 그를 백성들 앞에 끌어내 공개적으로 재판할 생각이었습니다.

v.CNV: 彼得就这样被拘留在监里；但教会却为他迫切地祈求　神。
v.ESV: So Peter was kept in prison, but earnest prayer for him was made to God by the church.
v.GAE: 이에 베드로는 옥에 갇혔고 교회는 그를 위하여 간절히 하나님께 기도하더라
v.KJV: Peter therefore was kept in prison: but prayer was made without ceasing of the church unto God for him.
v.KRV: 이에 베드로는 옥에 갇혔고 교회는 그를 위하여 간절히 하나님께 빌더라
v.NASB: So Peter was kept in the prison, but prayer for him was being made fervently by the church to God.
v.NIV: So Peter was kept in prison, but the church was earnestly praying to God for him.
v.NRSV: While Peter was kept in prison, the church prayed fervently to God for him.
v.SAENEW: 이렇게 되어서, 베드로가 감옥에 갇히고, 교회는 그를 위하여 하나님께 간절히 기도하였다.
v.WLB: 이렇게 베드로는 감옥에 갇혔고 교회는 그를 위해 하나님께 간절히 기도했습니다.

v.CNV: 希律要提他出来的前一夜，彼得被两条锁链锁住，睡在两个士兵中间，还有卫兵守在门前。
v.ESV: Now when Herod was about to bring him out, on that very night, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and sentries before the door were guarding the prison.
v.GAE: 헤롯이 잡아 내려고 하는 그 전날 밤에 베드로가 두 군인 틈에서 두 쇠사슬에 매여 누워 자는데 파수꾼들이 문 밖에서 옥을 지키더니
v.KJV: And when Herod would have brought him forth, the same night Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains: and the keepers before the door kept the prison.
v.KRV: 헤롯이 잡아 내려고 하는 그 전날 밤에 베드로가 두 군사 틈에서 두 쇠사슬에 매여 누워 자는데 파숫군들이 문 밖에서 옥을 지키더니
v.NASB: On the very night when Herod was about to bring him forward, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and guards in front of the door were watching over the prison.
v.NIV: The night before Herod was to bring him to trial, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and sentries stood guard at the entrance.
v.NRSV: The very night before Herod was going to bring him out, Peter, bound with two chains, was sleeping between two soldiers, while guards in front of the door were keeping watch over the prison.
v.SAENEW: 헤롯이 베드로를 백성들 앞에 끌어내기로 한 그 전날 밤이었다. 베드로는 두 쇠사슬에 묶여, 군인 두 사람 틈에서 잠들어 있었고, 문 앞에는 파수꾼들이 감옥을 지키고 있었다.
v.WLB: 헤롯이 베드로를 재판장으로 끌어내려고 하던 전날 밤, 베드로는 두 개의 쇠사슬에 묶인 채 두 명의 군인들 사이에서 잠들어 있었고 경비병들이 감옥 문을 지키고 서 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 忽然，有主的一位使者出现，屋就光芒四射。天使拍拍彼得的肋旁，唤醒他，说：“快起来！”他手上的锁链就脱落了。
v.ESV: And behold, an angel of the Lord stood next to him, and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side and woke him, saying, “Get up quickly.” And the chains fell off his hands.
v.GAE: 홀연히 주의 사자가 나타나매 옥중에 광채가 빛나며 또 베드로의 옆구리를 쳐 깨워 이르되 급히 일어나라 하니 쇠사슬이 그 손에서 벗어지더라
v.KJV: And, behold, the angel of the Lord came upon him, and a light shined in the prison: and he smote Peter on the side, and raised him up, saying, Arise up quickly. And his chains fell off from his hands.
v.KRV: 홀연히 주의 사자가 곁에 서매 옥중에 광채가 조요하며 또 베드로의 옆구리를 쳐 깨워 가로되 급히 일어나라 하니 쇠사슬이 그 손에서 벗어지더라
v.NASB: And behold, an angel of the Lord suddenly appeared and a light shone in the cell; and he struck Peter's side and woke him up, saying, 'Get up quickly.' And his chains fell off his hands.
v.NIV: Suddenly an angel of the Lord appeared and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side and woke him up. “Quick, get up!” he said, and the chains fell off Peter’s wrists.
v.NRSV: Suddenly an angel of the Lord appeared and a light shone in the cell. He tapped Peter on the side and woke him, saying, “Get up quickly.” And the chains fell off his wrists.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 갑자기 주님의 천사가 나타나고, 감방에 빛이 환히 비치었다. 천사가 베드로의 옆구리를 쳐서 깨우고 말하기를 "빨리 일어서라" 하였다. 그러자 쇠사슬이 그의 두 손목에서 풀렸다.
v.WLB: 그런데 갑자기 주의 천사가 나타나더니 감방에 빛이 환하게 비치는 것이었습니다. 그가 베드로의 옆구리를 찔러 깨우며 “어서 일어나거라” 하고 말했습니다. 그러자 베드로의 손목에 매여 있던 쇠사슬이 풀렸습니다.

v.CNV: 天使对他说：“束上带子，穿上鞋！”他就这样作了。天使又说：“披上外衣，跟我走！”
v.ESV: And the angel said to him, “Dress yourself and put on your sandals.” And he did so. And he said to him, “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.”
v.GAE: 천사가 이르되 띠를 띠고 신을 신으라 하거늘 베드로가 그대로 하니 천사가 또 이르되 겉옷을 입고 따라오라 한대
v.KJV: And the angel said unto him, Gird thyself, and bind on thy sandals. And so he did. And he saith unto him, Cast thy garment about thee, and follow me.
v.KRV: 천사가 가로되 띠를 띠고 신을 들메라 하거늘 베드로가 그대로 하니 천사가 또 가로되 겉옷을 입고 따라 오라 한대
v.NASB: And the angel said to him, 'Gird yourself and put on your sandals.' And he did so. And he said to him, 'Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.'
v.NIV: Then the angel said to him, “Put on your clothes and sandals.” And Peter did so. “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me,” the angel told him.
v.NRSV: The angel said to him, “Fasten your belt and put on your sandals.” He did so. Then he said to him, “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.”
v.SAENEW: 천사가 베드로에게 "띠를 띠고, 신을 신어라" 하고 말하니, 베드로가 그대로 하였다. 또 천사가 그에게 "겉옷을 두르고, 나를 따라오너라" 하니,
v.WLB: 그때 천사가 베드로에게 “허리띠를 매고 신을 신어라” 하고 말하자 베드로는 그렇게 했습니다. 천사가 다시 “겉옷을 입고 나를 따라오너라”고 말했습니다.

v.CNV: 他就出来跟着天使走，但他不知道天使所作的事是真的，还以为是见了异象。
v.ESV: And he went out and followed him. He did not know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
v.GAE: 베드로가 나와서 따라갈새 천사가 하는 것이 생시인 줄 알지 못하고 환상을 보는가 하니라
v.KJV: And he went out, and followed him; and wist not that it was true which was done by the angel; but thought he saw a vision.
v.KRV: 베드로가 나와서 따라갈쌔 천사의 하는 것이 참인줄 알지 못하고 환상을 보는가 하니라
v.NASB: And he went out and continued to follow, and he did not know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
v.NIV: Peter followed him out of the prison, but he had no idea that what the angel was doing was really happening; he thought he was seeing a vision.
v.NRSV: Peter went out and followed him; he did not realize that what was happening with the angel’s help was real; he thought he was seeing a vision.
v.SAENEW: 베드로가 감방에서 나와서, 천사를 따라갔다. 베드로는 천사가 하는 일이 참인 줄 모르고, 자기가 환상을 보고 있는 것이라고 생각하였다.
v.WLB: 베드로는 천사를 따라 감옥에서 나오면서도 도대체 천사가 하는 일이 꿈인지 생시인지 알 수 없었습니다. 그저 환상인 것만 같았습니다.

v.CNV: 他们经过第一、第二两个岗位，来到通往城内的铁门，那门自动给他们开了。他们出来，往前走了一条街，天使立刻离开了他。
v.ESV: When they had passed the first and the second guard, they came to the iron gate leading into the city. It opened for them of its own accord, and they went out and went along one street, and immediately the angel left him.
v.GAE: 이에 첫째와 둘째 파수를 지나 시내로 통한 쇠문에 이르니 문이 저절로 열리는지라 나와서 한 거리를 지나매 천사가 곧 떠나더라
v.KJV: When they were past the first and the second ward, they came unto the iron gate that leadeth unto the city; which opened to them of his own accord: and they went out, and passed on through one street; and forthwith the angel departed from him.
v.KRV: 이에 첫째와 둘째 파수를 지나 성으로 통한 쇠문에 이르니 문이 절로 열리는지라 나와 한 거리를 지나매 천사가 곧 떠나더라
v.NASB: When they had passed the first and second guard, they came to the iron gate that leads into the city, which opened for them by itself; and they went out and went along one street, and immediately the angel departed from him.
v.NIV: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself, and they went through it. When they had walked the length of one street, suddenly the angel left him.
v.NRSV: After they had passed the first and the second guard, they came before the iron gate leading into the city. It opened for them of its own accord, and they went outside and walked along a lane, when suddenly the angel left him.
v.SAENEW: 그들이 첫째 초소와 둘째 초소를 지나서, 시내로 통하는 철문에 이르니, 문이 저절로 열렸다. 그래서 그들은 바깥으로 나와서, 거리를 하나 지났다. 그 때에 갑자기 천사가 떠나갔다.
v.WLB: 그들은 첫 번째 경비병들과 두 번째 경비병들을 지나서 성으로 통하는 철문에 이르렀습니다. 그 문이 저절로 열려 그들은 바깥으로 나왔습니다. 거리를 하나 지나자 갑자기 천사가 떠나갔습니다.

v.CNV: 彼得清醒过来，说：“现在我确实知道，主差他的天使来，救我脱离希律的手和犹太人所期望的一切。”
v.ESV: When Peter came to himself, he said, “Now I am sure that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from the hand of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting.”
v.GAE: 이에 베드로가 정신이 들어 이르되 내가 이제야 참으로 주께서 그의 천사를 보내어 나를 헤롯의 손과 유대 백성의 모든 기대에서 벗어나게 하신 줄 알겠노라 하여
v.KJV: And when Peter was come to himself, he said, Now I know of a surety, that the Lord hath sent his angel, and hath delivered me out of the hand of Herod, and from all the expectation of the people of the Jews.
v.KRV: 이에 베드로가 정신이 나서 가로되 내가 이제야 참으로 주께서 그의 천사를 보내어 나를 헤롯의 손과 유대 백성의 모든 기대에서 벗어나게 하신줄 알겠노라 하여
v.NASB: When Peter came to himself, he said, 'Now I know for sure that the Lord has sent forth His angel and rescued me from the hand of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting.'
v.NIV: Then Peter came to himself and said, “Now I know without a doubt that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from Herod’s clutches and from everything the Jewish people were hoping would happen.”
v.NRSV: Then Peter came to himself and said, “Now I am sure that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from the hands of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting.”
v.SAENEW: 그 때에야 베드로가 정신이 나서 말하였다. "이제야 참으로 알겠다. 주님께서 주님의 천사를 보내셔서, 헤롯의 손에서, 그리고 유대 백성이 꾸민 모든 음모에서, 나를 건져 주셨다."
v.WLB: 그제야 비로소 베드로는 정신이 들어 “이제야 분명히 알겠다. 주께서 천사를 보내 헤롯의 손아귀와 유대 사람들의 모든 기대에서 나를 구해 주셨다”고 말했습니다.

v.CNV: 他明白了之后，就到约翰（别名马可）的母亲马利亚家里去；有许多人聚集在那里祷告。
v.ESV: When he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose other name was Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.
v.GAE: 깨닫고 마가라 하는 요한의 어머니 마리아의 집에 가니 여러 사람이 거기에 모여 기도하고 있더라
v.KJV: And when he had considered the thing, he came to the house of Mary the mother of John, whose surname was Mark; where many were gathered together praying.
v.KRV: 깨닫고 마가라 하는 요한의 어머니 마리아의 집에 가니 여러 사람이 모여 기도하더라
v.NASB: And when he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John who was also called Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.
v.NIV: When this had dawned on him, he went to the house of Mary the mother of John, also called Mark, where many people had gathered and were praying.
v.NRSV: As soon as he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose other name was Mark, where many had gathered and were praying.
v.SAENEW: 이런 사실을 깨닫고서, 베드로는, 마가라고도 하는 요한의 어머니 마리아의 집으로 갔다. 거기에는 많은 사람이 모여서 기도하고 있었다.
v.WLB: 이 사실을 깨달은 후 그는 마가라고도 하는 요한의 어머니 마리아의 집으로 갔습니다. 많은 사람들이 그곳에 모여 기도하고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 彼得敲了大门，有一个名叫罗大的使女，出来应门。
v.ESV: And when he knocked at the door of the gateway, a servant girl named Rhoda came to answer.
v.GAE: 베드로가 대문을 두드린대 로데라 하는 여자 아이가 영접하러 나왔다가
v.KJV: And as Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a damsel came to hearken, named Rhoda.
v.KRV: 베드로가 대문을 두드린대 로데라 하는 계집 아이가 영접하러 나왔다가
v.NASB: When he knocked at the door of the gate, a servant-girl named Rhoda came to answer.
v.NIV: Peter knocked at the outer entrance, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer the door.
v.NRSV: When he knocked at the outer gate, a maid named Rhoda came to answer.
v.SAENEW: 베드로가 대문을 두드리니, 로데라는 어린 여종이 맞으러 나왔다.
v.WLB: 베드로가 대문을 두드리자 로데라는 어린 여종이 문을 열어 주러 나왔습니다.

v.CNV: 她认出是彼得的声音，欢喜到顾不得开门，就跑进去报告，说彼得站在门外。
v.ESV: Recognizing Peter's voice, in her joy she did not open the gate but ran in and reported that Peter was standing at the gate.
v.GAE: 베드로의 음성인 줄 알고 기뻐하여 문을 미처 열지 못하고 달려 들어가 말하되 베드로가 대문 밖에 섰더라 하니
v.KJV: And when she knew Peter’s voice, she opened not the gate for gladness, but ran in, and told how Peter stood before the gate.
v.KRV: 베드로의 음성인줄 알고 기뻐하여 문을 미처 열지 못하고 달려 들어가 말하되 베드로가 대문 밖에 섰더라 하니
v.NASB: When she recognized Peter's voice, because of her joy she did not open the gate, but ran in and announced that Peter was standing in front of the gate.
v.NIV: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed she ran back without opening it and exclaimed, “Peter is at the door!”
v.NRSV: On recognizing Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed that, instead of opening the gate, she ran in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
v.SAENEW: 그 여종은 베드로의 목소리를 알아듣고, 너무 기뻐서, 문을 열지도 않고 도로 달려들어가서, 대문 앞에 베드로가 서 있다고 알렸다.
v.WLB: 여종은 베드로의 목소리인 줄 알아차리고 너무나 기뻐서 문 여는 것도 잊은 채 달려가 베드로가 문 밖에 와 있다고 소리쳤습니다.

v.CNV: 大家说：“你疯了！”她却坚持地说这是真的。他们说：“一定是他的天使。”
v.ESV: They said to her, “You are out of your mind.” But she kept insisting that it was so, and they kept saying, “It is his angel!”
v.GAE: 그들이 말하되 네가 미쳤다 하나 여자 아이는 힘써 말하되 참말이라 하니 그들이 말하되 그러면 그의 천사라 하더라
v.KJV: And they said unto her, Thou art mad. But she constantly affirmed that it was even so. Then said they, It is his angel.
v.KRV: 저희가 말하되 네가 미쳤다 하나 계집 아이는 힘써 말하되 참말이라 하니 저희가 말하되 그러면 그의 천사라 하더라
v.NASB: They said to her, 'You are out of your mind!' But she kept insisting that it was so. They kept saying, 'It is his angel.'
v.NIV: “You’re out of your mind,” they told her. When she kept insisting that it was so, they said, “It must be his angel.”
v.NRSV: They said to her, “You are out of your mind!” But she insisted that it was so. They said, “It is his angel.”
v.SAENEW: 사람들이 여종에게 "네가 미쳤구나" 하고 말하자, 여종은 참말이라고 우겼다. 그러자 그들은 "베드로의 천사일거야" 하고 말하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 사람들이 여종에게 “네가 미쳤구나”라고 말했습니다. 그러나 여종이 계속 사실이라고 우겨대자 그들은 “베드로의 천사겠지”라고 말했습니다.

v.CNV: 彼得继续敲门；他们打开了，一见是他，就非常惊讶。
v.ESV: But Peter continued knocking, and when they opened, they saw him and were amazed.
v.GAE: 베드로가 문 두드리기를 그치지 아니하니 그들이 문을 열어 베드로를 보고 놀라는지라
v.KJV: But Peter continued knocking: and when they had opened the door, and saw him, they were astonished.
v.KRV: 베드로가 문 두드리기를 그치지 아니하니 저희가 문을 열어 베드로를 보고 놀라는지라
v.NASB: But Peter continued knocking; and when they had opened the door, they saw him and were amazed.
v.NIV: But Peter kept on knocking, and when they opened the door and saw him, they were astonished.
v.NRSV: Meanwhile Peter continued knocking; and when they opened the gate, they saw him and were amazed.
v.SAENEW: 그 동안에 베드로가 줄곧 문을 두드리니, 사람들이 문을 열어서 베드로를 보고, 깜짝 놀랐다.
v.WLB: 그때 베드로가 계속 문을 두드렸습니다. 그들이 문을 열고 베드로를 보더니 깜짝 놀랐습니다.

v.CNV: 彼得作了一个手势，要他们安静，然后对他们述说主怎样领他出监，又说：“你们把这些事告诉雅各和众弟兄。”就离开那里，到别的地方去了。
v.ESV: But motioning to them with his hand to be silent, he described to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he said, “Tell these things to James and to the brothers.” Then he departed and went to another place.
v.GAE: 베드로가 그들에게 손짓하여 조용하게 하고 주께서 자기를 이끌어 옥에서 나오게 하던 일을 말하고 또 야고보와 형제들에게 이 말을 전하라 하고 떠나 다른 곳으로 가니라
v.KJV: But he, beckoning unto them with the hand to hold their peace, declared unto them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he said, Go shew these things unto James, and to the brethren. And he departed, and went into another place.
v.KRV: 베드로가 저희에게 손짓하여 종용하게 하고 주께서 자기를 이끌어 옥에서 나오게 하던 일을 말하고 또 야고보와 형제들에게 이 말을 전하라 하고 떠나 다른 곳으로 가니라
v.NASB: But motioning to them with his hand to be silent, he described to them how the Lord had led him out of the prison. And he said, 'Report these things to James and the brethren.' Then he left and went to another place.
v.NIV: Peter motioned with his hand for them to be quiet and described how the Lord had brought him out of prison. “Tell James and the other brothers and sisters about this,” he said, and then he left for another place.
v.NRSV: He motioned to them with his hand to be silent, and described for them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he added, “Tell this to James and to the believers.” Then he left and went to another place.
v.SAENEW: 베드로는 손을 흔들어서 그들을 조용하게 하고, 주님께서 자기를 감옥에서 인도하여 내신 일을 이야기하였다. 그리고 그는 "이 사실을 야고보와 다른 신도들에게 알리시오" 하고 말하고는, 거기에서 떠나 다른 곳으로 갔다.
v.WLB: 베드로는 조용히 하라고 손짓하며 주께서 자신을 감옥 밖으로 끌어내신 경위를 설명했습니다. “이 사실을 야고보와 그 형제들에게 알려 주시오”라고 말하고서 베드로는 다른 곳으로 떠났습니다.

v.CNV: 天亮的时候，士兵们非常慌乱，不知彼得出了什么事。
v.ESV: Now when day came, there was no little disturbance among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
v.GAE: 날이 새매 군인들은 베드로가 어떻게 되었는지 알지 못하여 적지 않게 소동하니
v.KJV: Now as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers, what was become of Peter.
v.KRV: 날이 새매 군사들은 베드로가 어떻게 되었는지 알지 못하여 적지 않게 소동하니
v.NASB: Now when day came, there was no small disturbance among the soldiers as to what could have become of Peter.
v.NIV: In the morning, there was no small commotion among the soldiers as to what had become of Peter.
v.NRSV: When morning came, there was no small commotion among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
v.SAENEW: 날이 새니, 군인들 사이에서는 베드로가 없어진 일로 작지 않은 소동이 일어났다.
v.WLB: 아침이 되자 베드로가 없어진 일로 인해 군인들 사이에는 적지 않은 소동이 벌어졌습니다.

v.CNV: 希律搜索他，却找不到，就审问卫兵，下令把他们带出去处死。后来希律离开犹太，到该撒利亚去，住在那里。
v.ESV: And after Herod searched for him and did not find him, he examined the sentries and ordered that they should be put to death. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea and spent time there.
v.GAE: 헤롯이 그를 찾아도 보지 못하매 파수꾼들을 심문하고 죽이라 명하니라 헤롯이 유대를 떠나 가이사랴로 내려가서 머무니라
v.KJV: And when Herod had sought for him, and found him not, he examined the keepers, and commanded that they should be put to death. And he went down from Judaea to Caesarea, and there abode.
v.KRV: 헤롯이 그를 찾아도 보지 못하매 파숫군들을 심문하고 죽이라 명하니라 헤롯이 유대를 떠나 가이사랴로 내려가서 거하니라
v.NASB: When Herod had searched for him and had not found him, he examined the guards and ordered that they be led away to execution. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea and was spending time there.
v.NIV: After Herod had a thorough search made for him and did not find him, he cross-examined the guards and ordered that they be executed. Then Herod went from Judea to Caesarea and stayed there.
v.NRSV: When Herod had searched for him and could not find him, he examined the guards and ordered them to be put to death. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea and stayed there.
v.SAENEW: 헤롯은 샅샅이 찾아보았으나, 베드로를 찾지 못하고, 경비병들을 문초한 뒤에, 명령을 내려서 그들을 사형에 처하였다. 그런 다음에, 헤롯은 유대를 떠나 가이사랴로 내려가서, 거기에서 한동안 지냈다.
v.WLB: 헤롯이 샅샅이 뒤져 보았지만 베드로를 찾을 수 없게 되자 헤롯은 경비병들을 심문한 뒤 그들을 대신 처형하라고 명령했습니다. 그 후 헤롯은 유대를 떠나 가이사랴로 가서 잠시 그곳에서 지냈습니다.

v.CNV: 当时希律对推罗和西顿人怀怒在心。这两地的人因为他们的地区都需要从王的领土得到粮食，就先拉拢了王的内侍臣伯拉斯都，然后同心地去见希律，要向他求和。
v.ESV: Now Herod was angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon, and they came to him with one accord, and having persuaded Blastus, the king's chamberlain, they asked for peace, because their country depended on the king's country for food.
v.GAE: 헤롯이 두로와 시돈 사람들을 대단히 노여워하니 그들의 지방이 왕국에서 나는 양식을 먹는 까닭에 한마음으로 그에게 나아와 왕의 침소 맡은 신하 블라스도를 설득하여 화목하기를 청한지라
v.KJV: And Herod was highly displeased with them of Tyre and Sidon: but they came with one accord to him, and, having made Blastus the king’s chamberlain their friend, desired peace; because their country was nourished by the king’s country.
v.KRV: 헤롯이 두로와 시돈 사람들을 대단히 노여워하나 저희 지방이 왕국에서 나는 양식을 쓰는고로 일심으로 그에게 나아와 왕의 침소 맡은 신하 블라스도를 친하여 화목하기를 청한지라
v.NASB: Now he was very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon; and with one accord they came to him, and having won over Blastus the king's chamberlain, they were asking for peace, because their country was fed by the king's country.
v.NIV: He had been quarreling with the people of Tyre and Sidon; they now joined together and sought an audience with him. After securing the support of Blastus, a trusted personal servant of the king, they asked for peace, because they depended on the king’s country for their food supply.
v.NRSV: Now Herod was angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. So they came to him in a body; and after winning over Blastus, the king’s chamberlain, they asked for a reconciliation, because their country depended on the king’s country for food.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 두로와 시돈 사람들은 헤롯에게 몹시 노여움을 사고 있었다. 그래서 그들은 뜻을 모아서, 왕을 찾아갔다. 그들은 왕의 침실 시종 블라스도를 설득하여, 그를 통해서 헤롯에게 화평을 청하였다. 그들의 지방이 왕의 영토에서 식량을 공급받고 있었으므로, 이렇게 할 수밖에 없었다.
v.WLB: 당시 헤롯은 두로와 시돈 사람들을 못마땅하게 여기고 있었습니다. 그래서 그들은 무리를 지어 헤롯에게 와서 왕의 시종 블라스도를 설득해 화친을 요청했습니다. 이것은 자기들이 헤롯 왕의 영토에서 나는 양식을 공급받고 있기 때문이었습니다.

v.CNV: 到了定好的日期，希律穿上王服，坐在高台上，向他们演讲。
v.ESV: On an appointed day Herod put on his royal robes, took his seat upon the throne, and delivered an oration to them.
v.GAE: 헤롯이 날을 택하여 왕복을 입고 단상에 앉아 백성에게 연설하니
v.KJV: And upon a set day Herod, arrayed in royal apparel, sat upon his throne, and made an oration unto them.
v.KRV: 헤롯이 날을 택하여 왕복을 입고 위에 앉아 백성을 효유한대
v.NASB: On an appointed day Herod, having put on his royal apparel, took his seat on the rostrum and began delivering an address to them.
v.NIV: On the appointed day Herod, wearing his royal robes, sat on his throne and delivered a public address to the people.
v.NRSV: On an appointed day Herod put on his royal robes, took his seat on the platform, and delivered a public address to them.
v.SAENEW: 지정된 날에, 헤롯이 용포를 걸쳐 입고, 왕좌에 좌정하여 그들에게 연설하였다.
v.WLB: 약속한 날에 헤롯은 왕의 의복을 갖춰 입고 왕좌에 앉아 사람들에게 연설을 했습니다.

v.CNV: 群众大声说：“这是　神的声音，不是人的声音！”
v.ESV: And the people were shouting, “The voice of a god, and not of a man!”
v.GAE: 백성들이 크게 부르되 이것은 신의 소리요 사람의 소리가 아니라 하거늘
v.KJV: And the people gave a shout, saying, It is the voice of a god, and not of a man.
v.KRV: 백성들이 크게 부르되 이것은 신의 소리요 사람의 소리는 아니라 하거늘
v.NASB: The people kept crying out, 'The voice of a god and not of a man!'
v.NIV: They shouted, “This is the voice of a god, not of a man.”
v.NRSV: The people kept shouting, “The voice of a god, and not of a mortal!”
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 군중이 "신의 소리다. 사람의 소리가 아니다" 하고 외쳤다.
v.WLB: 그러자 사람들은 “이것은 신의 음성이지 사람의 음성이 아니다”라고 외쳤습니다.

v.CNV: 他不归荣耀给　神，所以主的使者立刻击打他，他被虫咬，就断了气。
v.ESV: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck him down, because he did not give God the glory, and he was eaten by worms and breathed his last.
v.GAE: 헤롯이 영광을 하나님께로 돌리지 아니하므로 주의 사자가 곧 치니 벌레에게 먹혀 죽으니라
v.KJV: And immediately the angel of the Lord smote him, because he gave not God the glory: and he was eaten of worms, and gave up the ghost.
v.KRV: 헤롯이 영광을 하나님께로 돌리지 아니하는고로 주의 사자가 곧 치니 충이 먹어 죽으니라
v.NASB: And immediately an angel of the Lord struck him because he did not give God the glory, and he was eaten by worms and died.
v.NIV: Immediately, because Herod did not give praise to God, an angel of the Lord struck him down, and he was eaten by worms and died.
v.NRSV: And immediately, because he had not given the glory to God, an angel of the Lord struck him down, and he was eaten by worms and died.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 즉시로 주님의 천사가 헤롯을 내리쳤다. 헤롯이 하나님께 영광을 돌리지 않았기 때문이다. 그는 벌레에게 먹혀서 죽고 말았다.
v.WLB: 그런데 헤롯이 하나님께 영광을 돌리지 않았기 때문에 주의 천사가 즉시 헤롯을 내리쳐서 그는 벌레에 먹혀 죽고 말았습니다.

v.CNV: 神的道日渐兴旺，越发广传。
v.ESV: But the word of God increased and multiplied.
v.GAE: 하나님의 말씀은 흥왕하여 더하더라
v.KJV: But the word of God grew and multiplied.
v.KRV: 하나님의 말씀은 흥왕하여 더하더라
v.NASB: But the word of the Lord continued to grow and to be multiplied.
v.NIV: But the word of God continued to spread and flourish.
v.NRSV: But the word of God continued to advance and gain adherents.
v.SAENEW: 하나님의 말씀이 점점 더 널리 퍼지고, 믿는 사람이 많아졌다.
v.WLB: 그러나 하나님의 말씀은 점점 널리 퍼져서 믿는 사람이 더욱 늘어나게 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 巴拿巴和扫罗完成了送交捐项的任务，就带着名叫马可的约翰，从耶路撒冷回来。
v.ESV: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem when they had completed their service, bringing with them John, whose other name was Mark.
v.GAE: 바나바와 사울이 부조하는 일을 마치고 마가라 하는 요한을 데리고 예루살렘에서 돌아오니라
v.KJV: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem, when they had fulfilled their ministry, and took with them John, whose surname was Mark.
v.KRV: 바나바와 사울이 부조의 일을 마치고 마가라 하는 요한을 데리고 예루살렘에서 돌아오니라
v.NASB: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem when they had fulfilled their mission, taking along with them John, who was also called Mark.
v.NIV: When Barnabas and Saul had finished their mission, they returned from Jerusalem, taking with them John, also called Mark.
v.NRSV: Then after completing their mission Barnabas and Saul returned to Jerusalem and brought with them John, whose other name was Mark.
v.SAENEW: 바나바와 사울은 그들의 사명을 마치고, 마가라고도 하는 요한을 데리고 예루살렘에서 돌아왔다.
v.WLB: 바나바와 사울이 자기 임무를 마치자 그들은 마가라고도 하는 요한을 데리고 예루살렘에서 돌아왔습니다.
